methods.description: This method involves selecting and marking anatomical points on pollen grains to analyze morphological variation using digital tools and statistical software. The analysis aims to identify significant differences between taxonomic categories using PCA and CVA.

steps.description: Sections of the pollen grains that showed the greatest morphological variation were selected and these areas were marked with anatomic points or landmarks.
steps.searchable_summary: select and mark anatomical points on pollen grains

steps.description: Three photographs were used for each pollen grain, from which x, y coordinates were digitized using the TPSDIG program.
steps.searchable_summary: digitize x, y coordinates from pollen grain images using TPSDIG

steps.description: Conformational differences were analyzed in MorphoJ software using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) as an exploratory technique to visualize the axes of greatest variation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on digitized coordinates using MorphoJ

steps.description: Canonical Variables Analysis (CVA) was used to evaluate significant differences between the different groups (taxonomic categories).
steps.searchable_summary: perform CVA to evaluate differences between taxonomic groups
